title: census task in server.rs uses unrestricted reqwest client (no SSRF guard)
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:164-181

FINDING: The periodic census registration task (spawned inside serve()) builds a fresh reqwest::Client with only a timeout — no SSRF-safe resolver, no redirect policy. It then constructs a URL from the operator-configured domain string: `format!("https://the-federation.info/register/{census_domain}")`. If the operator sets domain to a value containing path traversal characters (e.g. "../../etc/passwd" or "internal-host.local") the URL sent to the-federation.info would be malformed. More relevantly, the client itself has no SSRF guard; if this code path is ever extended to fetch a response body or follow the domain value to another URL, it bypasses all SSRF protections.

Separately: the census domain is embedded directly into the URL path without URL-encoding. A domain containing "/" would inject path segments into the census URL.

IMPACT: Operator-controlled configuration issue (domain is operator-set, not user-controlled), so direct exploitation requires a misconfigured or compromised config file. The issue is that the client architecture is inconsistent: three different clients with three different security properties exist in the binary, making the security model hard to reason about and audit.
